checks.question: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?

findings.detail: The user’s added scope note about markdown-rendering resource descriptions is only partially reflected in the plan. The repo does have the exact plain-text `ResourceModal` block the note calls out at `banodoco-website/src/pages/Resources/ResourceModal.tsx:186-189`, but the plan never adds a concrete step for that rendering change or the requested success criterion; it only mentions using the existing markdown renderer for comment content.

One of the explicit edge cases in the brief, attachment removal on Discord edit, is still unresolved in the plan. The proposed schema for `asset_comment_media` only has `(comment_id, media_id, sort_order)` and no visibility/deleted flag, so the plan’s note that it 'could mark `is_deleted` on junction' is not actually backed by any migration or follow-up step.

checks.question: Are the proposed changes technically correct?

findings.detail: The cursor strategy in the promoter is not technically correct for the stated trigger. `discord_messages.reaction_count` is a denormalized field that gets recomputed from `discord_reactions` by the `sync_message_reactors` trigger (`supabase/migrations/20260319100000_fix_sync_reactors_trigger.sql:1-22`), so an older thread can cross the 5-reaction threshold long after its `created_at`. A promoter that only scans `created_at > cursor` will permanently miss those late-promoted OPs once the cursor advances past their creation time.

The fallback member-write described in Phase 2 would violate the current schema. `members` is the renamed `discord_members` table, and its original definition still requires `username TEXT NOT NULL` (`supabase/migrations/20251101000000_create_discord_tables.sql:38-64`); the archive writer in `brain-of-bndc/src/common/storage_handler.py:190-209` also always supplies a username. The plan’s 'insert only member_id, username=NULL' fallback would fail the FK-prep path instead of rescuing it.

The proposed asset type mapping (`lora` or `tool`) does not match the website’s current resource model. Manual resource creation only offers `lora` and `workflow` (`banodoco-website/src/pages/SubmitResource/index.tsx:10-13`), and the cards/filters branch on `asset.type === 'workflow'` (`ResourceModal.tsx:151-156`, `ResourceCard.tsx:151-154`, `useResourceFilters.ts:159-160`). Importing Discord rows with `type='tool'` would render them as LoRAs in some places and make them unfilterable in others.

checks.question: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?

findings.detail: The plan scopes the frontend work around `ResourceModal`, but `rg -n "ResourceModal" banodoco-website/src` only finds the component definition and no callers. The live resource surface is `banodoco-website/src/pages/ResourceDetail/index.tsx`, which still renders `resource.description` as plain text at lines 166-169 and already owns the visible 'Made with this' gallery. Limiting the change to the dead modal leaves the actual resource page behavior unchanged.

The broader 'resource visibility' surface is larger than the plan accounts for. Resource lists and profile counts currently come from `useResources.ts`, `useCommunityResources.ts`, `useCommunityResource.ts`, and `useUserProfile.ts`, all of which filter only on `admin_status` and never consider a future `assets.is_hidden` kill switch (`useResources.ts:51-74`, `useCommunityResources.ts:118-127`, `useCommunityResource.ts:103-113`, `useUserProfile.ts:125-133`). If `is_hidden` is meant to hide imported assets globally, the change needs to reach those callers too, not just one modal/card.

checks.question: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?

findings.detail: The plan does not thread the new asset metadata through the existing data loaders that would need it for the requested UI. `useResources.ts`, `useCommunityResources.ts`, and `useCommunityResource.ts` currently select neither `source` nor `discord_channel_id` nor `is_hidden` (`useResources.ts:51-65`, `useCommunityResources.ts:118-124`, `useCommunityResource.ts:103-109`), so the proposed 'From Discord' badge and hide switch cannot work until those queries and their TypeScript shapes are expanded.

The media-import plan also skips over existing Cloudflare support that already owns part of this lifecycle. The repo has `cloudflare-stream-webhook` for writing HLS/thumbnail URLs from a Stream UID and `cloudflare-stream-backfill` for retrying unfinished ingests (`supabase/functions/cloudflare-stream-backfill/index.ts:35-114`), but the new plan introduces a separate importer that polls Cloudflare to readiness itself. That duplication is a supporting-infrastructure gap unless the plan explicitly says which path becomes canonical and how the existing webhook/backfill jobs are reused or retired.

The cron registration snippet assumes a secret-delivery pattern that is not present elsewhere in the repo. Existing pg_cron usage schedules local SQL directly (`supabase/migrations/20260403000005_schedule_priority_scores_cron.sql:4-23`), and `supabase/config.toml:45-46` only contains a commented `db.vault` stub. A migration that references `current_setting('app.settings.service_role_key')` without first establishing that setting will not be self-contained.

checks.question: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?

findings.detail: I traced the current callers that consume resource rows, and they all pass through the existing hooks with narrow assumptions about the payload: `useResources.ts` reads `id, type, name, description, admin_status, ...`, `useCommunityResources.ts` reads `id, name, description, type, lora_link, ...`, and `useCommunityResource.ts` reads `id, name, description, type, ...` (`useResources.ts:51-65`, `useCommunityResources.ts:118-124`, `useCommunityResource.ts:103-109`). None of those callers request `source`, `discord_channel_id`, or `is_hidden`, so the downstream badge, jump-link, and hide logic proposed in the plan would have no inputs unless the callers themselves are revised.

I also checked the direct callers of the UI surface the plan wants to edit. `rg -n "ResourceModal" banodoco-website/src` returns only the component definition and no imports/usages, which means there are currently no callers passing assets into that modal at all. The meaningful caller path today is the route-driven `ResourceDetail` page, so the proposed fix is landing at the wrong boundary unless the plan also restores or introduces actual `ResourceModal` usage.

flags.concern: Discord resource promoter: created_at-based cursors will miss older threads that cross the reaction threshold after the cursor has advanced.
flags.evidence: The plan proposes qualifying OP discovery with `created_at > cursor`, but `discord_messages.reaction_count` is updated later by the `sync_message_reactors` trigger in `supabase/migrations/20260319100000_fix_sync_reactors_trigger.sql:1-22`.

flags.concern: Discord member fallback: the proposed thin upsert is incompatible with the current members schema because username is still required.
flags.evidence: `discord_members` was created with `username TEXT NOT NULL` in `supabase/migrations/20251101000000_create_discord_tables.sql:38-64`, then renamed to `members`; the archive writer also always supplies username in `brain-of-bndc/src/common/storage_handler.py:190-209`.

flags.concern: Resource typing: importing assets as `tool` conflicts with the website’s existing `lora`/`workflow` assumptions.
flags.evidence: `SubmitResource` only offers `lora` and `workflow` (`banodoco-website/src/pages/SubmitResource/index.tsx:10-13`), and filters/cards branch on `asset.type === "workflow"` in `ResourceModal.tsx`, `ResourceCard.tsx`, and `useResourceFilters.ts`.

flags.concern: Resource frontend surface: the plan targets an effectively unused modal and does not cover the route-driven ResourceDetail page that users actually hit.
flags.evidence: `rg -n "ResourceModal" banodoco-website/src` finds only the component definition, while `banodoco-website/src/pages/ResourceDetail/index.tsx:166-169` still renders resource descriptions as plain text and owns the active gallery UI.

flags.concern: Edge scheduling/infrastructure: the plan introduces new cron auth and Cloudflare polling paths without reconciling existing pg_cron and Stream webhook/backfill patterns.
flags.evidence: Current cron usage is direct SQL in `supabase/migrations/20260403000005_schedule_priority_scores_cron.sql:4-23`; `supabase/config.toml:45-46` has no active vault setup; and the repo already ships `cloudflare-stream-webhook` plus `cloudflare-stream-backfill` for Stream completion/retry handling.
